video.title: ICE COLD Esteban Chaves Attacks the INEOS Train | Volta a Catalunya Stage 4 2021

transcript.clean_text: welcome back to the lantern rouge youtube channel the highlights of stage four the volta catalunya 2021 the last mountain test with ineos in the driver's seat with riders on first second and fourth in the general classification this stage was hundred and sixty-seven kilometers long from ripple to portney finishing at about 2000 meters altitude two category one climbs the first of which in the middle of the stage was not particularly steep just very very long the port del canto and then the final climb was 18 and a half case at 6.6 with quite a steep section in the last 1500 meters with ineos dominating gc already and none of the other teams realistically having any threats to put pressure on them in the mountains it was definitely a stage the break could have won and in cycling we really see the battle at the start of these mountain stages for the break to form bora trying to get zv off and shelling in the break toll look for jumbo visma there dan martin and walt poles and ineos were policing the front with row and dennis trying to see who's getting to the break how close are they on gc we happy with that breakaway eventually one did form with tolhuk uran attila volta etc but the big problem was leonard kemner on borah hans grower had got into that breakaway only a minute and 30 back on gc from adam yates so ineos had to chase pretty much all day leonard camden will not be on lucrow's christmas list i don't think i'm gonna attack the break early on that second long climb and then castro viejo was pacing for ineos with their full train set up on that club he had a gap of about three minutes 33 minutes 40 seconds chris fern beginning to drop halfway up that climb with the altitude hangover from apres ski at valtteri earlier in the month for the other gc contenders there really wasn't much they could do simon yates hasn't showed anywhere neither level of form he had at toronto adriatico on the praty de tivo stage so kemna crested that second climb with about a three minute gap the break was somewhere in no man's land between him and the peloton led by ineos behind they had a pretty quick highway descent before the last climate portenae there was a short valley as well before that climb where ineos with row and dennis waiting in the wings would surely put a lot of time into leonard kemna and that's really been the story of this race ineos controlling the stages exactly as they want they've got the best climber in adam yates and they've got easily the strongest team so they cleaned up that main breakaway group in the valley before that final porting a climb and they didn't actually catch leonard canada before the climb but rowan dennis was way fresher and brought back cameron after about four k's adam yates immediately getting on the blower and now the question was what would ineos do they've brought back leonard cam now we heard from dave brailsford last year that they want to ride in this new exciting fashion when you've got the two-time world champion and the tt climbing like this and then the next man to pull a turn who's not even in contention for gc is richard karapan's winner of the year of italia then it's choo choo train time because adam yates is 50 seconds plus ahead on gc there's no need to make this stage any more complicated than it has to be and the train is the most effective way of keeping all the other gc contenders from trying to do anything and all they can do with a slight headwind on this climb is just sit behind this ineos train and hope they don't get dropped until richard karapas probably the most exciting part of the middle of this climb he miss shifted dropped his chain and we thought oh no what what's going to happen with grant thomas richie port and adam yates if they're going to have to sacrifice one of their gc positions but thomas pretty much just rode tempo on the front whilst richard karapas quickly got his chain back on and sauntered back up to the group until stephen chris spike who didn't look good on stage three at all attacked when carapaz was trying to move back up and sure i'd love to see it the pace had gone down and ineos were looking vulnerable perhaps for a period of 30 seconds but in hindsight yeah it wasn't the best idea for stefan crow's fight because kara pass just went straight back on the front and brought him back and then cryospike got dropped about three minutes later but the big move of the day was with seven and a half k's or so left on this climb esteban chavez coming from deep climb so well on the stage before attacking in the face of richard karapas and people saying oh karapa's not in great form well he pulled for like 10 k's or so on this climb today and kept it all together the ineos henrikh mas attacking with like two and a half three k's to go for mother star but still carapace still pulling at this point he kept the gap to chavez at no more than 25 27 seconds and now that karapas had left all the ineos gc contenders so close to the finish just over two k's to go port and thomas just rode their own selected pace to limit the gap to esteban chavez on gc they brought it back to about 13 seconds with 1k to go they brought back master then got dropped but ineos weren't interested in sacrificing one of their riders gc positions to bring back esteban chavez who's climbing better than we've seen him climb in almost two years his last win was at the giro d'italia in 2019 when he was out of gc contentions on one of the mountain stages and today he attacked in the face of the ineos trident who were just about seven seconds behind him at this point finally dropping almeida with 500 meters to go bringing thomas probably up into that third position on gc they couldn't catch esteban chavez great to see him back on form with that big smile on his face and it poses some difficult questions for bike exchange going into those grand tours chavez can you trust that he will continue this form simon yates is there a more inconsistent rider in the world tour anyway behind us amount of chavez the gc group was largely together but for our maida losing some time in the last 500 meters coos kelderman all there esteban chavez winning the stage seven seconds ahead of mike woods ahead of thomas third taking some more bonus seconds adam yates of course staying in the leaders jersey 45 seconds ahead of richie port 49 seconds ahead of guerrain thomas we now have an ineos one two three which is valverde 14 seconds behind thomas in fourth perhaps that could change if valverde has a really strong stage in stage seven on the monument circuit around barcelona let's hear from adam yates on this stage that didn't play out exactly as ineos wanted it to yeah it's a good day yeah we were happy with the break at the beginning and then uh i think it was cam that jumped across and uh he wasn't so fine down on gc so we had to pull all day and we had to pull uh harder than we wanted to uh so in the end it was a hard day uh not not just for the guys riding but just for you know everyone in the wheels as well as it was a really hard day but yeah in the end i just had to follow the guys they did a really good job and uh i just stopped there and uh went to the finish so uh all in all perfect day so i think ineos having to work a lot harder in this stage as adam yates said they didn't seem to be too fussed or he didn't about the stage when i definitely think adam yates could have taken this stage if he'd attacked on the final climb the fact that leonard cameron was up the road meant that they couldn't just let the break go which i think was their plan which plays into what i think will happen for the next stage in stage five which is 193 k's from la pablo de segura to manresa it doesn't really have too much for the gc contenders two main climbs the first 8ks at 5.2 percent and then the second 7.3 k is at 6.7 percent but it crests about 24 k's from the finish so i think ineos will be happy for a breakaway to go up the road and take this and before catalonia started i thought this breakaway was designed perfectly for mark here she now moved to uae team emirates he showed the tour de france last year that he's strong enough to attack breakaway companions on the climbs and then tt away to the finish but if he doesn't get the breakaway on stage five today i'll be very surprised and that'll be a big tale into what mark heushi's current form is like on this stage which i think should be a big target for borah hansgrove they haven't got too much out of this race so far i think they should have used shelling more as their main guy in stage one and i think he's a big contender for today's stage so look out for him if he gets in today's break but i hope you enjoyed the video like it down below if you did honestly i don't think there's too much any of the other teams can do to upset ineos or adam yates at this point but we can only hope going into the last few stages of this year's catalonia but i'll see you with the highlights of stage five tomorrow ciao
